mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in real retail sales reflects strengthening consumer demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Twenty-day forward returns following positive RSAFS growth months show non-positive average return or Rank IC below 0.03 over a three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above eight percent signals substantial labor market slack that suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 sixty-day forward returns are positive in more than fifty percent of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds eight percent over a three-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month expansion in industrial production alongside a federal funds rate below one percent signals an amplified real recovery supported by extraordinary monetary accommodation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO momentum and FEDFUNDS level produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any two-year rolling window, or underperforms a single-factor INDPRO model.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recoveries, rapid month-over-month industrial production growth signals capacity normalization and inventory rebuilding, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: In identified post-shock recovery periods, positive INDPRO momentum fails to coincide with positive average five-day SPX returns at a statistically significant level over a two-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding seven hundred thousand units per month indicate a durable recovery in residential investment and downstream demand for materials and labor, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds seven hundred, S&P 500 sixty-day forward returns are negative on average over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation reduces real discount rates and shifts investor preference toward nominal equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL momentum is followed by negative average twenty-day SPX returns over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS surprises coincide with negative average five-day SPX returns over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate pinned below twenty-five basis points commits the central bank to forward guidance and unconventional tools that suppress equity risk premia, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, S&P 500 sixty-day forward returns are not statistically different from zero over a three-year window using a t-test.
